lines: Hessa Marr wrote names where a road clerk would have written numbers. Candle grease stiffens the page edges.
The first leaf begins after the Stilling. Hessa crossed out the office heading, REMAINS RECEIVED, and wrote NAMES KEPT.
Twelve marks run down the inside cover. The last was pressed so hard that the paper split.
I asked Eren if the stone still hurt. Two cracks came through the chair beneath my hand, and I did not ask him again.
Lysa heard the bell while the rope was still in my fist. She struck her teeth until I cut out the clapper.
Ilyen knew my step. The ash moved at his feet before I touched the gate.
Margin cuts beside Maud show a broken circle. Toma is marked by opened ribs; Lysa by the bell mouth.
A final page names Sava Rell. Hessa wrote that the wardens took him below the washing house because he turned north before Hallowfen's bells sounded.

effects.log: Hessa kept twelve names. Only eleven remain in the graveyard.

choices.label: Set Hessa's record beside the docket

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: find Sava Rell and decide what remains.

effects.log: Names and subject numbers join. Sava answered Hallowfen before the road bells rang.

lines: The forced stone tore several leaves from the binding. Hessa's cramped hand survives along the inner margins.
I asked Eren if the stone still hurt. Two cracks came through the chair beneath my hand, and I did not ask him again.
Lysa heard the bell while the rope was still in my fist. She struck her teeth until I cut out the clapper.
Ilyen knew my step. The ash moved at his feet before I touched the gate.
One inner margin survives intact. Maud bears a broken circle, Toma opened ribs, and Lysa the bell mouth.
Sava Rell's page is split, but the last sentence remains: the wardens took him below after he turned north before the Hallowfen bells.

effects.log: The damaged register still keeps twelve names.

choices.label: Set the torn record beside the docket

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: find Sava Rell and decide what remains.

effects.log: Enough survives to join names with numbers. Sava answered Hallowfen before the road bells rang.

lines: Hessa's book keeps twelve names. Sava Rell is the only one without a place in the graveyard.
The removed clapper lies beside the entry where Lysa heard a bell that had not yet moved.
